We could talk about this month's referral contest. So that's a really good spot to do it at the top of page three. Sean, can you talk about, can you, let me just interrupt you there. Can you talk, give us some, I know it's kind of outside the scope of, you know, putting the newsletter together. But you've seen a lot of referral contacts. Could you just give the person listening some idea of something that they could do for a referral contest? Yeah, so, you know, it's hard because it depends on the value of an average client, right? But, so let me give you two or three examples here. We see, a lot of times we see the contest where it's some kind of gift or prize, which always works better than money, by the way. So, you know, win this iPad. You know, we'll do a drawing, win an iPad. Sometimes it's everybody wins something and it's a smaller prize. And so when we do everyone win something, everyone wants to do like, hey, here's $100 cash or here's $100 Visa gift card. That's, again, money. It doesn't work as well. It's not as motivating. You would think that would be the opposite, but it really isn't. People want stuff. And so, if you're doing kind of, you know, a little bit more on the, if your budget's a little on the smaller end for this, one good prize that we've seen work really well is a date night. And you just go down to Costco. You buy the, you know, like $100 worth of gift certificates to the local restaurant. They usually have a nice restaurant there. And it's like $80 for $100 worth of gift certificates. And you buy a pair of movie tickets and send this referral this month and get a date night. And so that's a relatively inexpensive one. It's costing like $60 a referral. But people really like that. It's a very popular one. You can do the prizes, like I said, where, you know, there's a big prize and you do a drawing. When you do that, I really have found that it's best that everyone wins a small something. And then one person wins a big something. We've even seen to expand on the date night option where everyone wins the date night. But one person gets a limo sent to pick them up. And it's for four people and not two people. So they get to bring a friend. And then they go out on a great date, maybe even to a nicer restaurant or whatever. And then the limo brings them back. And so that's, you know, that's kind of the big prize. So everyone gets date night and someone gets the ultimate date night. And that's not that expensive to do. We just rented a limo for my son to go to prom. And I think it was like, you know, $250 box or something like that. It wasn't, it's, you know, it's not super expensive. And if you get enough referrals, you're dividing that up amongst a bunch of people. The referral contest, if you've got, like if you're business to business and your clients are a little bit larger, I'll tell you what we're doing. And you can find some variation of this to work for you, I'm sure. But we're doing a contest where if you refer two people, and that's what we're looking for, because this is a bigger prize, but you get your, you get to have an experience in Las Vegas. So we're paying to fly you to Vegas. We're paying for your hotel. We're giving you some money. We're taking out the dinner, you know, one or two nights. I don't remember which one it is. And then the experience is your choice of experience. You can either A, come out to the racetrack and pick from some luxury sports cars like Ferraris and Lamborghinis and Porsches and stuff. And you get five laps on the Las Vegas Speedway racetrack in each car. So you get two cars, five laps in each car. Or we've got a backstage VIP experience to Cirque du Soleil's oh show where you get, we get VIP seating, we just go backstage and meet the people and kind of the performers and stuff and see how it's all done and all that kind of great stuff. And so, you know, we have a choose your experience promotion. And we will run that for, you know, that'll be something we run out for through the end of the year. And then we'll go sometime, you know, beginning part of next year. And then the way we did it was if you can't make it, we will basically, you know, give you the value and cash type of a deal. So because there will be some people who are like, I can't make it in February. It's, you know, February. That's awesome. I mean, that's just great. I mean, that's just great. And just as a little twist on the first thing that you said, the other thing you could do with which I've done with the client is the restaurant one, you could, if you have enough subscribers to your newsletter, enough people you're sending the newsletter to, you can often go to the local restaurant and they'll give you the certificate to give out. And if they're smart, yeah, because you're promoting them in the newsletter. And also, they're getting people to come in the restaurant. So, but Sean, you're right. The easiest way to do it is to screw that and just go buy it. But, you know, working out joint ventures with other local businesses.
